conditions.message: The staged documentation adds local operating standards to a single orchestrator sidecar file. Some wording intentionally overlaps existing orchestration guidance, but it is consolidated into topic-specific sections for the active repo and does not introduce duplicated logic, parallel implementations, or copy-pasted tests.
evidence: git diff --cached --name-status shows one staged file: ORCHESTRATOR.md modified.
git diff --cached --stat shows 72 insertions in ORCHESTRATOR.md and no other staged payload paths.
The additions are grouped into distinct sections: Monitoring Discipline, Polling Standard, Model And Effort Tiering, and Agent Mail Registration.
The repeated concepts are intentional repo-local operating standards for the orchestrator, not executable duplicate behavior or alternate implementations.

conditions.message: The staged standards directly reduce superficial completion signals by requiring artifact-based polling, receipt confirmation, pane/model provenance, Agent Mail identities, and explicit timeout or dispatch decisions instead of trusting scrollback, wakes, or idle panes as success.
evidence: Monitoring Discipline requires dispatching the next unblocked unit after every gate or explicitly recording why none is dispatchable.
The stale-scrollback rule says the artifact, not the pane tail, is the completion signal, and requires two idle polls plus verification against findings, responses, or inbox artifacts.
Receipt confirmation requires confirming sends or wakes via ntm tail or attention events, stating that an unconfirmed send is not a dispatch.
Model And Effort Tiering requires deliberate spawn-time effort selection, dispatch-note recording, and no mid-verification tier changes.

conditions.message: The staged documentation touches coordination and audit-boundary policy and narrows it by requiring Agent Mail registration, contact-mesh preapproval, provenance recording, and state verification. It introduces no secrets, auth flow, tenant/PII handling, sandbox permission, or external API behavior changes.
evidence: Agent Mail Registration requires every codex sub-agent in the NTM session to register with project key /home/ubuntu/Projects/burpvalve before starting work and to include program, model, and assigned task description.
The contact-mesh rule requires pre-approving expected communication paths so finished work is not blocked behind pending contact approval.
The NTM evidence rule requires snapshot evidence before and after state-changing NTM actions and treats missing after-state as unverified.

The staged diff is documentation-only in ORCHESTRATOR.md and contains no credential values, credential storage changes, auth code, tenant or PII data handling, network client changes, or sandbox policy code.
